Meta.Title: 3-Day Soccer Agility Training Plan (Beginner-Intermediate, 75 Minutes per Day)
Meta.Summary: Three 75-minute sessions focused on soccer-specific agility: acceleration/deceleration, change of direction, footwork, reaction, and ball-integrated agility. Includes warm-up, progressive main blocks, conditioning dose, and cooldown/mobility. Designed for a busy beginner-intermediate adult with emphasis on safe loading and measurable progress.

Overview.Focus: Agility (change of direction, deceleration, reactive movement, footwork, and ball-integrated agility)
Overview.WeeklyFlow: Day 1: Mechanics and fundamentals. Day 2: Reactive agility and decision making. Day 3: Game-like agility under fatigue.
Overview.Equipment: Soccer ball, 8-12 cones, 4 mini-hurdles (optional), agility ladder (optional), stopwatch/phone timer, wall/rebound surface or partner (optional).

IntensityGuide.Rule: Quality first: stop a rep if form breaks.
IntensityGuide.RPEScale: RPE 1-10. Agility reps typically RPE 7-9 with full recovery; conditioning block RPE 6-8.
IntensityGuide.RecoveryBetweenReps: 20-60 seconds depending on drill; longer for high-speed COD.

SafetyNotes.Item: Train on flat, non-slip surface; wear supportive footwear.
Prioritize deceleration: hips back, knees track over toes, chest tall, short steps before plant.
If any sharp pain (especially knee/ankle/groin), stop and modify to lower speed or reduce angles.

ProgressTracking.Metric: Best time and average time for 5-10-5 shuttle (Day 1 and Day 3).
Number of clean reps (no cone hits, controlled stop) for COD drills.
Perceived quickness and fatigue (RPE) after conditioning block.

Day.Title: Agility Fundamentals: Footwork, Deceleration, and Controlled COD
Day.Goal: Build movement quality: stance, braking, and efficient change of direction at moderate to high speed.

Block.name: General pulse raiser
Block.Drill: Easy jog or jump rope 2 minutes, then alternating: 20 seconds high knees, 20 seconds butt kicks, 20 seconds lateral shuffle, 20 seconds backpedal, repeat.

Block.name: Dynamic mobility
Block.Drill: Walking lunges with rotation x 10/side, leg swings (front/back and side) x 10/side, ankle rocks x 10/side, hip openers x 8/side.

Block.name: Activation and prep
Block.Drill: Glute bridge x 10, side plank x 20 seconds/side, calf raises x 15, single-leg balance with slight knee bend x 20 seconds/side.

Drill.name: A-skip to 5m acceleration
Drill.Prescription: 3 sets: A-skip 10m then accelerate 5m; walk back recovery.
Drill.CoachingCues: Drive knees up, punch down, tall posture; accelerate smoothly.

Drill.name: Deceleration drops
Drill.Prescription: 4 reps: build to 70 percent over 10m, then decelerate to a complete stop within 3-4 steps; 45-60 seconds rest.
Drill.CoachingCues: Lower hips, short steps, knees over toes, do not collapse inward.

Drill.name: 5-10-5 shuttle (pro agility) - practice
Drill.Setup: 3 cones in a line, 5 yards between cones.
Drill.Prescription: 2 warm-up reps at 60-70 percent, then 4 timed reps at 80-90 percent; rest 90 seconds between reps.
Drill.CoachingCues: Powerful first step, drop center of mass before plant, push the ground away.

Drill.name: L-drill (3-cone) - controlled
Drill.Setup: Cones in L shape: 5 yards up, 5 yards across.
Drill.Prescription: 3 reps each direction at 70-85 percent; rest 60-90 seconds.
Drill.CoachingCues: Stay low through turns; keep eyes up; smooth transitions.

Drill.name: Lateral cone line quick steps
Drill.Setup: 6 cones spaced 1 yard apart in a straight line.
Drill.Prescription: 3 sets: shuffle down and back with quick feet; 45 seconds rest.
Drill.Progression: Increase speed while maintaining hip control and no crossing feet.

Drill.name: In-and-out box steps (no ladder needed)
Drill.Setup: Create a 1x1 yard square with 4 cones.
Drill.Prescription: 3 sets x 20 seconds work / 40 seconds rest (forward, lateral, then diagonal patterns).

Drill.name: Cone slalom with controlled turns
Drill.Prescription: 6 runs total: 3 right-foot emphasis, 3 left-foot emphasis; walk back recovery.
Drill.CoachingCues: Small touches, hips low, accelerate out of final cone.

Block.name: Easy downshift
Block.Drill: Easy jog to walk, deep breathing.

Block.name: Mobility and tissue care
Block.Drill: Hip flexor stretch 45 seconds/side, adductor rock-backs x 10/side, calf stretch 45 seconds/side, hamstring stretch 45 seconds/side.

Notes.Item: If time is tight, keep the 5-10-5 shuttle and deceleration drops as priorities.
Record best and average shuttle times for comparison on Day 3.

Day.Title: Reactive Agility: Stimulus-Response, Quick Reorientation, and Ball Cues
Day.Goal: Improve reaction speed and re-acceleration using visual or auditory cues; add soccer-specific scanning and ball control.

Block.name: General warm-up
Block.Drill: Jog 2 minutes, then 3 rounds: 20 seconds lateral shuffle, 20 seconds carioca, 20 seconds backpedal, 20 seconds fast feet in place.

Block.name: Dynamic mobility
Block.Drill: Worlds greatest stretch x 5/side, groin opens (cossack to stand) x 6/side, ankle hops in place x 20.

Block.name: Neural priming
Block.Drill: 4 reps: 5m burst from athletic stance; 30-45 seconds rest.
2 sets x 8/side; focus on soft landing and knee alignment.

Drill.name: Mirror shuffle (partner) or phone cue shuffle (solo)
Drill.Setup: Mark a 5m wide lane with cones.
Drill.Prescription: 6 rounds x 20 seconds work / 40 seconds rest.

Options.Option: Partner: mirror their lateral movements without crossing feet.
Solo: use random timer beeps; on beep change direction.

Drill.CoachingCues: Stay low, quick feet, hips square, do not stand upright when changing.

Drill.name: Reactive decel to turn
Drill.Setup: One start cone and one stop cone 10m away; place left and right turn cones 2m behind stop cone at 45 degrees.
Drill.Prescription: 8 reps: accelerate to stop cone; on a cue (left/right) cut to the indicated cone; rest 60 seconds.
Drill.CoachingCues: Brake first, then cut; avoid long reaching plant.

Drill.name: T-drill with callouts
Drill.Setup: T shape: sprint up 10m, shuffle 5m left, shuffle 10m right, shuffle 5m left to center, backpedal 10m.
Drill.Prescription: 5 reps total at 80-90 percent; rest 90 seconds.
Drill.Variation: At the top, have a partner call "left" or "right" first; if solo, pre-set a randomizer (coin flip) before each rep.

Drill.name: 4-cone reaction box
Drill.Setup: Square 6x6m, start in center with 4 numbered cones.
Drill.Prescription: 3 sets: 8 calls per set (touch called cone and return center); rest 90 seconds between sets.
Drill.CoachingCues: Short steps into the turn, head up, accelerate out.

Drill.name: Pass and cut (wall or partner)
Drill.Setup: Stand 5-7m from wall/partner; two cones 3m left and right of your start spot.
Drill.Prescription: 3 sets x 6 reps: pass, then immediately cut to a called cone (or alternate L/R), return, receive next ball; 60-90 seconds rest.
Drill.CoachingCues: Scan before receiving, first touch sets next movement, stay on toes.

Drill.name: Dribble-reaction gates
Drill.Setup: Place 6-8 small gates (two cones 1m apart) in a 12x12m area.
Drill.Prescription: 4 rounds x 60 seconds: dribble and go through as many gates as possible; on random cue change gate direction; 60 seconds rest.
Drill.CoachingCues: Keep ball close; accelerate after each gate; use both feet.

Block.name: Breathing and reset
Block.Drill: Walk and nasal breathing; lower heart rate.

Block.name: Mobility
Block.Drill: Adductor stretch 45 seconds/side, quad stretch 45 seconds/side, glute stretch 45 seconds/side, gentle ankle mobility x 10/side.

Notes.Item: Keep reactive drills crisp; if reaction slows, extend rest rather than pushing sloppy reps.
For solo training, use random audio cues from a timer app and pre-assign directions (odd beep = left, even beep = right).

Day.Title: Agility Under Fatigue: Repeated COD, Transition Speed, and Small-Sided Patterns
Day.Goal: Maintain agility and decision quality when tired; emphasize repeated accelerations, braking, and quick transitions similar to match play.

Block.name: General warm-up
Block.Drill: Easy jog 2 minutes, then 2 rounds: 20 seconds skipping, 20 seconds shuffle, 20 seconds backpedal, 20 seconds fast feet.

Block.name: Dynamic mobility
Block.Drill: Leg swings x 10/side, lateral lunges x 8/side, hamstring scoops x 8/side, ankle hops x 20.

Block.name: Priming
Block.Drill: 3 reps: 20m progressive run to 85 percent; walk back.
2 reps: accelerate 10m, controlled stop in 3-4 steps.

Drill.name: 5-10-5 shuttle (pro agility) - retest
Drill.Prescription: 1 rep at 70 percent, then 3 timed reps at 85-95 percent; rest 90-120 seconds.
Drill.Goal: Match or beat Day 1 average with clean turns.

Drill.name: COD intervals (15s on / 45s off)
Drill.Setup: Set 3 cones in a triangle, each side about 6-8m.
Drill.Prescription: 2 sets x 6 reps: 15 seconds continuous sprint/shuffle between cones with sharp turns, 45 seconds rest; 2 minutes rest between sets.
Drill.CoachingCues: Stay compact into turns, accelerate out, control breathing during rest.

Drill.name: Sprint-brake-sprint
Drill.Prescription: 6 reps: sprint to 10m, hard brake, re-accelerate to 15m; walk back recovery (60-75 seconds).

Drill.name: 1v0 change-of-direction dribble circuit
Drill.Setup: Four cones: start, two middle cones 5m apart, finish cone 10m from start (zig-zag route).
Drill.Prescription: 8 total runs: 4 with right-foot turns emphasized, 4 with left-foot turns emphasized; rest 45-60 seconds.
Drill.Constraints: On each turn, use an outside cut on one rep and an inside cut on the next.

Drill.name: Transition touches
Drill.Prescription: 5 rounds x 45 seconds: dribble through one gate, turn quickly, accelerate to the other gate; 45 seconds rest.
Drill.CoachingCues: Explode after the turn; keep touches purposeful (not too many).

Drill.name: Reactive burst series
Drill.Prescription: 5 reps: from athletic stance, respond to random cue and burst 5m (left/right/forward), stop under control; 30-45 seconds rest.

Block.name: Mobility and recovery
Block.Drill: Hip flexor stretch 60 seconds/side, adductor stretch 60 seconds/side, calves 45 seconds/side, gentle thoracic rotation x 6/side.

Notes.Item: If shuttle times drop more than about 5 percent across reps, increase rest or stop the speed work and move to ball control at lower intensity.
For a time-efficient PhD schedule, keep Day 3 as the hardest day and schedule a full rest or light recovery day afterward.

WeekToWeek.Item: Add 1 rep per key drill (shuttle, T-drill, COD intervals) or increase speed slightly, not both in the same week.
Reduce rest by 5-10 seconds only after you can keep clean form and stable knee alignment.
Increase cutting angles gradually (45 degrees to 90 degrees) as control improves.
